titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L1100
GA L1100
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: K13V3 at S5W1D5b changed to K13V1 by Rachel Kevern.
19.30-2 block marker moved forwards to after περι αυτου by Rachel Kevern.
Ghost pages removed by Catherine Smith.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 D1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-A number: L 1100 (Athos Laura A 102)Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1r (folios missing at beginning)Τranscribed by: RLMΤranscription begun: 14.08.02Τranscription finished:Τransference to Collate by: RLMΤransference to Collate begun: 14.08.02Τransference to Collate finished:File date and time: Ρlaced in Level1: Level1 file date and time:Ρlaced in Level2: read a second time, compared with the collation by Joannes Karavidopoulos, and differences resolvedΡlaced in Level2 by: RLMLevel2 file date and time: 21.09.02Τhese files were compared and differences resolved by:Ρlaced in Level3:Ρlaced in Level3 by:Final transcription file date and time: Page layout added by: acmStart date: 16.10.13End date: 23.10.13Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No. Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No. Other remarks:Unidentified reading 1 is on F149r: John 3,13-17.

div.note: John 6,35-39 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D6)
John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)

ab.note: These are instructions to locate the reading for S1 W3 D7: John 15,17-16,2 σαββατω γ.

div.note: John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman

ab.note: A dedication is added in a small hand: του αγιου κωνσταντινουπολεως.
A title is added in a small hand: εις εγκαινια.
A title has been added in the top margin: εις τον ορθρον της υψωσεως, and in the text is added in a small hand: ειπεν ο κς̅.

div.note: John 14,1-11a (Scrivener John 14,1-10; Gale John 14,1-11, Wake John 14,1-12)
John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)

ab.note: These are instructions to locate the reading for S1 W8 D1a: John 20,19-23.

div.note: John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτουργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
This lection has no heading and follows straight on from Matthew.
John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)
This lection has no heading and follows straight on from Matthew.
The heading instructs the user to read this pericope on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.

ab.note: These are instructions to locate the reading for M1 D14a: John 12, 28-36.
